messages.content: Build a pure Decider for a Loan aggregate in TypeScript. Commands: originate, disburse, close. Disbursing an already-closed loan must throw a typed DomainError. Export initialState, decide, evolve.
Explain briefly how a Decider models a Mortgage aggregate, then implement it in TypeScript with strict types. Commands: apply, approve, reject. Approving a rejected mortgage must throw.
Write a TypeScript Decider for an Asset aggregate. Constraints: strict types, no any, exhaustive never default, at least two invariant checks. Commands: register, revalue, retire.
Build a Portfolio Decider in TypeScript. Commands: open, addHolding, close. Closing a portfolio with non-zero holdings must throw.
What's the idiomatic way to build a marketing Campaign aggregate as a Decider in TypeScript? Commands: launch, pause, resume, end. Show a minimal but complete example.
Write an Audit Decider in TypeScript. Commands: start, recordFinding, conclude. Concluding before any findings must throw. Use strict types and an exhaustive never default.
Build a LoanBook projection in TypeScript that folds LoanOriginated, LoanDisbursed, and LoanClosed events into a flat view with id, principal, and status.
Write a Portfolio value-summary projection in TypeScript. Project PortfolioOpened, HoldingAdded, and PortfolioClosed events into a totalUnits view keyed by portfolio id. Ignore unrelated events.
Implement a Campaign performance read-model projection in TypeScript. Events: CampaignLaunched, ImpressionRecorded, ConversionRecorded. View carries impressions and conversions per campaign id.
Create an Audit trail projection in TypeScript. Project AuditStarted and FindingRecorded events into a map from audit id to a list of findings. AuditConcluded should mark the audit closed.

Implement a Snapshot with versioned upgrade for a Position aggregate in TypeScript. Include CURRENT_VERSION, two upgrader functions v1Tov2 and v2Tov3, and a loader that brings any stored Snapshot to the current state.

Refactor this ad-hoc Tranche snapshot loader into a proper versioned Snapshot upgrader in TypeScript with a current-version constant and a typed upgrader. Snippet: a plain object loader that blindly casts JSON to the current Tranche state.

Build a Snapshot layer for a Strategy aggregate in TypeScript that combines a versioned snapshot with a replay of newer events on top. Include CURRENT_VERSION, one upgrader, and an evolve reducer used by the loader.

Show how to use expectedRevision for optimistic concurrency when appending Loan events in TypeScript. Declare the EventStore interface locally and convert the store's conflict signal into a typed ConcurrencyConflict.
Wrap a Mortgage append with optimistic concurrency in an Effect.gen program. Declare the EventStore locally, use expectedRevision, and surface a ConcurrencyConflict tagged error.
Implement a retry wrapper in TypeScript that retries Portfolio appends on ConcurrencyConflict up to three times. Use a local EventStore interface and expectedRevision.
Build a CommandHandler for the Loan aggregate in TypeScript. It should load the stream, call decide, and append the new events with expectedRevision. Declare a local EventStore interface.
Build a Mortgage CommandHandler in TypeScript using Effect.gen. Load events, fold into state, decide, and appendToStream with expectedRevision.
Write a generic CommandHandler for the Asset aggregate in TypeScript. It should accept any Asset command, load the stream, fold with evolve, call decide, and append with expectedRevision.

Fix this Audit CommandHandler so it uses expectedRevision and exhaustive matching. Snippet: a handler that swallows decide errors and appends without revision checks. Produce a corrected full TypeScript file.
Write a Campaign read-model subscription wrapped in Effect.gen that resumes from the last checkpoint. Declare CheckpointStore locally. Crash-safe: checkpoint AFTER apply.
Implement a resumable subscription for a LoanBook projection in TypeScript. Keep track of the last processed position, apply the projection, then persist the checkpoint.
Wrap a Portfolio subscription loop in Effect.gen. Resume from checkpoint, apply projection, then persist and advance. Use local CheckpointStore interface.
Show the canonical aggregateStream helper in TypeScript and use it to reconstruct a Position aggregate from a list of events. Include a typed when function.
Build a when reducer and aggregateStream helper for a Holding aggregate in TypeScript. Events: HoldingRegistered, HoldingIncreased, HoldingReduced.
Implement a Report aggregate reconstructed with an Oskar-style when + aggregateStream in TypeScript. Events: ReportStarted, SectionAdded, ReportPublished.
Build an async aggregateStream helper in TypeScript that folds an AsyncIterable of events into a RiskLimit aggregate. Include a when reducer and a runtime check.
Show upcasting in TypeScript for Loan events: migrate a v1 LoanOriginated event that carried amount to the current shape that carries principal. Include an upcast function with version discriminator.
Write an upcast function in TypeScript that migrates old Campaign events. v1 CampaignLaunched had a title field; v2 renamed it to name. Produce the current event union.

Implement a read-model rebuild for the Audit trail projection in TypeScript. Read events in batches from a local event-store iterator, call project per event, persist per batch, and advance a revision cursor. Support a `from` resume option.
Write a chunked rebuild loop in TypeScript that rebuilds a Campaign performance projection from revision 0. Persist per batch, emit progress, and support resume via a `from` option.
Implement a minimal CQRS split for the Portfolio aggregate in TypeScript. Write path uses decide + appendToStream. Read path queries a separate read model. Share the event DU but not the state type.
Build a CQRS-shaped Asset module in TypeScript. handleCommand uses decide + appendToStream. queryView reads a denormalised read model. Include a comment about eventual consistency.

Write a stateless process-manager react function in TypeScript that reacts to Mortgage events: MortgageApplied triggers a RunCreditCheck command; CreditCheckPassed triggers an ApproveMortgage command. Return null for unrelated events.
Implement a 3-step saga in TypeScript: CampaignLaunched leads to AllocateBudget; BudgetAllocated leads to SendOpeningEmail; EmailSent returns null. Pure react function, strict types.

Show a domain-to-integration event translator in TypeScript for the Loan aggregate. Only LoanOriginated and LoanDisbursed become integration events; LoanClosed maps to null. Include a versioned schema field.

Implement an outbox pattern in TypeScript for Campaign events. appendWithOutbox should insert outbox rows and append to the stream in a single transaction. A separate publisher (commented, not implemented) polls the outbox and marks rows published.
Write a pure evolve reducer in TypeScript for a Tranche aggregate and a helper aggregate(events) that uses reduce<TrancheState>. Include an exhaustive never default.
Explain briefly how evolve differs from decide for a Holding aggregate, then show a pure evolve reducer in TypeScript with a never default.
What's the idiomatic way to reconstruct a Strategy aggregate's current state from its event stream in TypeScript? Show a minimal example using reduce and a typed evolve.
